1→//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n     2→//\n     3→//                         BusTub\n     4→//\n     5→// hash_util.h\n     6→//\n     7→// Identification: src/include/common/util/hash_util.h\n     8→//\n     9→// Copyright (c) 2015-2019, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n    10→//\n    11→//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n    12→\n    13→#pragma once\n    14→\n    15→#include <algorithm>\n    16→#include <cstdlib>\n    17→#include <iostream>\n    18→#include <string>\n    19→\n    20→#include \"common/macros.h\"\n    21→#include \"type/value.h\"\n    22→\n    23→namespace bustub {\n    24→\n    25→using hash_t = std::size_t;\n    26→\n    27→class HashUtil {\n    28→ private:\n    29→  static const hash_t PRIME_FACTOR = 10000019;\n    30→\n    31→ public:\n    32→  static inline auto HashBytes(const char *bytes, size_t length) -> hash_t {\n    33→    // https://github.com/greenplum-db/gpos/blob/b53c1acd6285de94044ff91fbee91589543feba1/libgpos/src/utils.cpp#L126\n    34→    hash_t hash = length;\n    35→    for (size_t i = 0; i < length; ++i) {\n    36→      hash = ((hash << 5) ^ (hash >> 27)) ^ bytes[i];\n    37→    }\n    38→    return hash;\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  static inline auto CombineHashes(hash_t l, hash_t r) -> hash_t {\n    42→    hash_t both[2] = {};\n    43→    both[0] = l;\n    44→    both[1] = r;\n    45→    return HashBytes(reinterpret_cast<char *>(both), sizeof(hash_t) * 2);\n    46→  }\n    47→\n    48→  static inline auto SumHashes(hash_t l, hash_t r) -> hash_t {\n    49→    return (l % PRIME_FACTOR + r % PRIME_FACTOR) % PRIME_FACTOR;\n    50→  }\n    51→\n    52→  template <typename T>\n    53→  static inline auto Hash(const T *ptr) -> hash_t {\n    54→    return HashBytes(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(ptr), sizeof(T));\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  template <typename T>\n    58→  static inline auto HashPtr(const T *ptr) -> hash_t {\n    59→    return HashBytes(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(&ptr), sizeof(void *));\n    60→  }\n    61→\n    62→  /** @return the hash of the value */\n    63→  static inline auto HashValue(const Value *val) -> hash_t {\n    64→    switch (val->GetTypeId()) {\n    65→      case TypeId::TINYINT: {\n    66→        auto raw = static_cast<int64_t>(val->GetAs<int8_t>());\n    67→        return Hash<int64_t>(&raw);\n    68→      }\n    69→      case TypeId::SMALLINT: {\n    70→        auto raw = static_cast<int64_t>(val->GetAs<int16_t>());\n    71→        return Hash<int64_t>(&raw);\n    72→      }\n    73→      case TypeId::INTEGER: {\n    74→        auto raw = static_cast<int64_t>(val->GetAs<int32_t>());\n    75→        return Hash<int64_t>(&raw);\n    76→      }\n    77→      case TypeId::BIGINT: {\n    78→        auto raw = static_cast<int64_t>(val->GetAs<int64_t>());\n    79→        return Hash<int64_t>(&raw);\n    80→      }\n    81→      case TypeId::BOOLEAN: {\n    82→        auto raw = val->GetAs<bool>();\n    83→        return Hash<bool>(&raw);\n    84→      }\n    85→      case TypeId::DECIMAL: {\n    86→        auto raw = val->GetAs<double>();\n    87→        return Hash<double>(&raw);\n    88→      }\n    89→      case TypeId::VARCHAR: {\n    90→        auto raw = val->GetData();\n    91→        auto len = val->GetStorageSize();\n    92→        return HashBytes(raw, len);\n    93→      }\n    94→      case TypeId::TIMESTAMP: {\n    95→        auto raw = val->GetAs<uint64_t>();\n    96→        return Hash<uint64_t>(&raw);\n    97→      }\n    98→      default: {\n    99→        UNIMPLEMENTED(\"Unsupported type.\");\n   100→      }\n   101→    }\n   102→  }\n   103→};\n   104→\n   105→}  // namespace bustub\n   106→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should This should find the position of the leftmost (most significant) 1 bit in the bitset. The position is counted from the right (position 0 is the rightmost), so this is basically 64 - leading_zeros - 1 (or we can think of it as 64 - leading_zeros if we're counting leading zeros plus 1 for the position of the first 1).\n\nActually, looking at HyperLogLog, the \"position of leftmost one\" typically means the position of the first 1 bit when reading from left to right. For a 64-bit value, if we have leftmost 1 at position X (0-indexed from the left), then the number of leading zeros is X. \n\nBut typically in HyperLogLog, we want to count leading zeros starting from the most significant bit. So if the first 1 is at position k from the right, we have 64-k-1 leading zeros (or 64-k if we count from the position after the leftmost 1).\n\nLet me think about this more carefully. In HyperLogLog:- We hash a value to get a hash value- We split the hash into two parts: the first n_bits are used as the bucket index- The remaining bits are used to find the position of the first 1 bit- This position is the \"rho\" value or the run length\n\nThe standard HyperLogLog algorithm finds the position of the first 1 bit in the remaining bits (after the bucket index bits). This is typically done by counting leading zeros and adding 1.\n\nActually, looking at the test cases:\n- Test with string \"Welcome to CMU DB (15-445/645)\" with n_bits=1 expects cardinality of 2\n- This suggests the algorithm is finding the number of leading zeros in the hash bits\n\nLet me implement PositionOfLeftmostOne to count leading zeros:","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":5,"cache_creation_input_tokens":318,"cache_read_input_tokens":46406,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":318},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e1165fff-f4db-4088-852f-61315f8b9558","uuid":"db339380-0551-4b89-a182-59984e0efeca"}

i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n      if (dense_bucket_[i].to_ullong() == 0 && overflow_bucket_.find(i) == overflow_bucket_.end()) {\n        zeros++;\n      }\n    }\n    if (zeros != 0) {\n      raw_estimate = m * std::log(static_cast<double>(m) / static_cast<double>(zeros));\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Large range correction\n  if (raw_estimate > (1.0 / 30.0) * (1UL << 32)) {\n    raw_estimate = -1.0 * (1UL << 32) * std::log(1.0 - raw_estimate / (1UL << 32));\n  }\n\n  cardinality_ = static_cast<uint64_t>(raw_estimate + 0.5); i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n      if (dense_bucket_[i].to_ullong() == 0 && overflow_bucket_.find(i) == overflow_bucket_.end()) {\n        zeros++;\n      }\n    }\n    if (zeros != 0) {\n      raw_estimate = m * std::log(static_cast<double>(m) / static_cast<double>(zeros));\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Large range correction\n  if (raw_estimate > (1.0 / 30.0) * (1UL << 32)) {\n    raw_estimate = -1.0 * (1UL << 32) * std::log(1.0 - raw_estimate / (1UL << 32));\n  }\n\n  cardinality_ = static_cast<uint64_t>(raw_estimate + 0.5);  // 2^n_bits, so 2^0=1, 2^1=2, etc.\n    registers_.assign(num_buckets_, 0);\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeBinary(const hash_t &hash) const -> std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> {\n  return std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY>(hash);\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> &bset) const -> uint64_t {\n  // Count leading zeros (from the most significant bit)\n  // and return the position of the first 1 bit\n  for (int64_t i = BITSET_CAPACITY - 1; i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n    raw_estimate += std::pow(2.0, -static_cast<double>(registers_[i]));\n  }\n\n  raw_estimate = CONSTANT * m * m / raw_estimate;\n\n  // Small range correction\n  if (raw_estimate <= 2.5 * m) {\n    size_t zeros = 0;\n    for (size_t i = 0; {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Usvzo9KLpsUBuistxqo9co","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#include \"primer/hyperloglog.h\"\n     2→\n     3→namespace bustub {\n     4→\n     5→template <typename KeyType>\n     6→HyperLogLog<KeyType>::HyperLogLog(int16_t n_bits)\n     7→    : cardinality_(0), n_bits_(n_bits) {\n     8→  if (n_bits_ < 0) {\n     9→    num_buckets_ = 0;\n    10→    registers_.clear();\n    11→  } else {\n    12→    num_buckets_ = 1UL << n_bits_;  // 2^n_bits, so 2^0=1, 2^1=2, etc.\n    13→    registers_.assign(num_buckets_, 0);\n    14→  }\n    15→}\n    16→\n    17→template <typename KeyType>\n    18→auto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeBinary(const hash_t &hash) const -> std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> {\n    19→  return std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY>(hash);\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→template <typename KeyType>\n    23→auto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> &bset) const -> uint64_t {\n    24→  // Count leading zeros (from the most significant bit)\n    25→  // and return the position of the first 1 bit\n    26→  for (int64_t i = BITSET_CAPACITY - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n    27→    if (bset[i]) {\n    28→      return BITSET_CAPACITY - i;  // Number of leading zeros + 1\n    29→    }\n    30→  }\n    31→  return BITSET_CAPACITY + 1;  // All zeros case\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→template <typename KeyType>\n    35→auto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::AddElem(KeyType val) -> void {\n    36→  if (num_buckets_ == 0) {\n    37→    return;\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(mutex_);\n    41→\n    42→  // Hash the value\n    43→  hash_t hash_val = CalculateHash(val);\n    44→\n    45→  // Extract bucket index from the LSBs (least significant bits)\n    46→  size_t bucket_idx = hash_val & ((1UL << n_bits_) - 1);\n    47→\n    48→  // Get the remaining bits after removing the bucket index bits\n    49→  hash_t remaining_bits = hash_val >> n_bits_;\n    50→\n    51→  // Convert to bitset and find position of leftmost 1\n    52→  auto bset = ComputeBinary(remaining_bits);\n    53→  uint64_t rho = PositionOfLeftmostOne(bset);\n    54→\n    55→  // Update the register with the maximum rho value\n    56→  if (rho > registers_[bucket_idx]) {\n    57→    registers_[bucket_idx] = rho;\n    58→  }\n    59→}\n    60→\n    61→template <typename KeyType>\n    62→auto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeCardinality() -> void {\n    63→  if (num_buckets_ == 0) {\n    64→    cardinality_ = 0;\n    65→    return;\n    66→  }\n    67→\n    68→  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(mutex_);\n    69→\n    70→  // HyperLogLog formula: E = constant * m^2 / sum(2^(-M[j]))\n    71→  double m = static_cast<double>(num_buckets_);\n    72→  double raw_estimate = 0.0;\n    73→\n    74→  for (size_t i = 0; i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n    75→    raw_estimate += std::pow(2.0, -static_cast<double>(registers_[i]));\n    76→  }\n    77→\n    78→  raw_estimate = CONSTANT * m * m / raw_estimate;\n    79→\n    80→  // Small range correction\n    81→  if (raw_estimate <= 2.5 * m) {\n    82→    size_t zeros = 0;\n    83→    for (size_t i = 0; i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n    84→      if (registers_[i] == 0) {\n    85→        zeros++;\n    86→      }\n    87→    }\n    88→    if (zeros != 0) {\n    89→      raw_estimate = m * std::log(static_cast<double>(m) / static_cast<double>(zeros));\n    90→    }\n    91→  }\n    92→\n    93→  // Large range correction\n    94→  if (raw_estimate > (1.0 / 30.0) * (1UL << 32)) {\n    95→    raw_estimate = -1.0 * (1UL << 32) * std::log(1.0 - raw_estimate / (1UL << 32));\n    96→  }\n    97→\n    98→  cardinality_ = static_cast<size_t>(raw_estimate + 0.5);  // Round to nearest integer\n    99→}\n   100→\n   101→template class HyperLogLog<int64_t>;\n   102→template class HyperLogLog<std::string>;\n   103→\n   104→}  // namespace bustub\n   105→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FSAQBE6tibVzU3NUbZSwxR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#include \"primer/hyperloglog_presto.h\"\n     2→\n     3→namespace bustub {\n     4→\n     5→template <typename KeyType>\n     6→HyperLogLogPresto<KeyType>::HyperLogLogPresto(int16_t n_leading_bits)\n     7→    : cardinality_(0), n_leading_bits_(n_leading_bits) {\n     8→  if (n_leading_bits_ < 0) {\n     9→    num_buckets_ = 0;\n    10→    dense_bucket_.clear();\n    11→    overflow_bucket_.clear();\n    12→  } else {\n    13→    num_buckets_ = 1UL << n_leading_bits_;  // 2^n_leading_bits, so 2^0=1, 2^1=2, etc.\n    14→    dense_bucket_.assign(num_buckets_, 0);\n    15→  }\n    16→}\n    17→\n    18→template <typename KeyType>\n    19→auto HyperLogLogPresto<KeyType>::AddElem(KeyType val) -> void {\n    20→  if (num_buckets_ == 0) {\n    21→    return;\n    22→  }\n    23→\n    24→  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(mutex_);\n    25→\n    26→  // Hash the value\n    27→  hash_t hash_val = CalculateHash(val);\n    28→\n    29→  // Extract bucket index from the first n_leading_bits\n    30→  size_t bucket_idx = hash_val & ((1UL << n_leading_bits_) - 1);\n    31→\n    32→  // Get the remaining bits after removing the bucket index bits\n    33→  hash_t remaining_bits = hash_val >> n_leading_bits_;\n    34→\n    35→  // Count leading zeros in the remaining bits\n    36→  uint64_t rho = 1;\n    37→  if (remaining_bits != 0) {\n    38→    rho = __builtin_clzll(remaining_bits) + 1;\n    39→  } else {\n    40→    rho = 65;  // All zeros case (64 leading zeros + 1)\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Store in dense bucket if rho <= DENSE_BUCKET_SIZE (4 bits can store 0-15)\n    44→  if (rho <= (1UL << DENSE_BUCKET_SIZE) - 1) {  // 15\n    45→    // Update dense bucket with max value\n    46→    if (rho > dense_bucket_[bucket_idx].to_ullong()) {\n    47→      dense_bucket_[bucket_idx] = std::bitset<DENSE_BUCKET_SIZE>(rho);\n    48→    }\n    49→  } else {\n    50→    // Store in overflow bucket\n    51→    if (overflow_bucket_.find(bucket_idx) == overflow_bucket_.end()) {\n    52→      overflow_bucket_[bucket_idx] = std::bitset<OVERFLOW_BUCKET_SIZE>(0);\n    53→    }\n    54→    // In overflow bucket, we need to track multiple values or just the max\n    55→    // Based on the test, it looks like we store the count or additional bits\n    56→    auto current = overflow_bucket_[bucket_idx].to_ullong();\n    57→    current++;\n    58→    overflow_bucket_[bucket_idx] = std::bitset<OVERFLOW_BUCKET_SIZE>(current);\n    59→  }\n    60→}\n    61→\n    62→template <typename T>\n    63→auto HyperLogLogPresto<T>::ComputeCardinality() -> void {\n    64→  if (num_buckets_ == 0) {\n    65→    cardinality_ = 0;\n    66→    return;\n    67→  }\n    68→\n    69→  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(mutex_);\n    70→\n    71→  // HyperLogLog formula: E = constant * m^2 / sum(2^(-M[j]))\n    72→  double m = static_cast<double>(num_buckets_);\n    73→  double raw_estimate = 0.0;\n    74→\n    75→  for (size_t i = 0; i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n    76→    uint64_t rho = dense_bucket_[i].to_ullong();\n    77→\n    78→    // Check if there's an overflow bucket value\n    79→    if (overflow_bucket_.find(i) != overflow_bucket_.end()) {\n    80→      // The overflow bucket stores additional rho value beyond the dense bucket\n    81→      uint64_t overflow_val = overflow_bucket_[i].to_ullong();\n    82→      // Use the max of dense and overflow\n    83→      rho = std::max(rho, overflow_val);\n    84→    }\n    85→\n    86→    // Clamp rho to reasonable values\n    87→    if (rho == 0) {\n    88→      rho = 1;\n    89→    }\n    90→\n    91→    raw_estimate += std::pow(2.0, -static_cast<double>(rho));\n    92→  }\n    93→\n    94→  raw_estimate = CONSTANT * m * m / raw_estimate;\n    95→\n    96→  // Small range correction\n    97→  if (raw_estimate <= 2.5 * m) {\n    98→    size_t zeros = 0;\n    99→    for (size_t i = 0; i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n   100→      if (dense_bucket_[i].to_ullong() == 0 && overflow_bucket_.find(i) == overflow_bucket_.end()) {\n   101→        zeros++;\n   102→      }\n   103→    }\n   104→    if (zeros != 0) {\n   105→      raw_estimate = m * std::log(static_cast<double>(m) / static_cast<double>(zeros));\n   106→    }\n   107→  }\n   108→\n   109→  // Large range correction\n   110→  if (raw_estimate > (1.0 / 30.0) * (1UL << 32)) {\n   111→    raw_estimate = -1.0 * (1UL << 32) * std::log(1.0 - raw_estimate / (1UL << 32));\n   112→  }\n   113→\n   114→  cardinality_ = static_cast<uint64_t>(raw_estimate + 0.5);  // Round to nearest integer\n   115→}\n   116→\n   117→template class HyperLogLogPresto<int64_t>;\n   118→template class HyperLogLogPresto<std::string>;\n   119→}  // namespace bustub\n   120→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AogTSksrDvKnRhLERSEDfK","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! I can see the issues now. The rho values are mostly 1, which confirms the problem. Let me trace through what's happening:\n\nThe issue is clearer now - when you extract the remaining bits and convert to bitset, the function is not correctly computing the position. The problem appears to be that the `PositionOfLeftmostOne` is working on a potentially very small number, and the bitset indexing might be off.\n\nLet me create a detailed analysis document with the correct algorithm:\n\n## Complete HyperLogLog Rho Algorithm Explanation\n\n### The Core Issue\n\nYour current implementation has a subtle bug. When you do:\n```cpp\nhash_t remaining_bits = hash_val >> n_bits_;\nauto bset = ComputeBinary(remaining_bits);\nuint64_t rho = PositionOfLeftmostOne(bset);\n```\n\nThe `remaining_bits` could be a very small number. When you convert it to a 64-bit bitset, the bits get positioned at the **rightmost** positions, not the leftmost. So when you iterate from position 63 down, you're looking at the wrong end of the number!\n\n### The Correct Approach\n\n**Option 1: Using Built-in Count Leading Zeros (Recommended)**\n\nThis is the standard approach used in production implementations:\n\n```cpp\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const hash_t &hash) const -> uint64_t {\n  // hash is the value AFTER removing bucket index bits\n  if (hash == 0) {\n    return 65;  // All zeros case\n  }\n  \n  // __builtin_clzll counts leading zeros in a uint64_t (64-bit value)\n  // Result is 0-63 for non-zero values\n  int leading_zeros = __builtin_clzll(hash);\n  \n  // Rho = position of first 1 bit = leading zeros + 1\n  return static_cast<uint64_t>(leading_zeros) + 1;\n}\n```\n\n**Option 2: Manual Bit Checking (without built-ins)**\n\n```cpp\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const hash_t &hash) const -> uint64_t {\n  if (hash == 0) {\n    return 65;\n  }\n  \n  // Start from the MSB (bit 63) and work down\n  uint64_t rho = 1;\n  uint64_t mask = (1ULL << 63);  // MSB\n  \n  while (!(hash & mask)) {\n    rho++;\n    mask >>= 1;\n  }\n  \n  return rho;\n}\n```\n\n### Why Your Current Code Is Wrong\n\nYour current code in `PositionOfLeftmostOne` works with a `bitset<64>`:\n```cpp\nfor (int64_t i = BITSET_CAPACITY - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n  if (bset[i]) {\n    return BITSET_CAPACITY - i;  // This formula is correct...\n  }\n}\n```\n\nBut the problem is you're **passing a bitset constructed from a shifted, small number**. The bitset doesn't preserve the \"position\" information - it just sets bits at indices 0, 1, 2, etc.\n\nFor example:\n- `remaining_bits = 5` (binary: ...00101)\n- `bset = bitset<64>(5)` → sets bits at positions [0] and [2]\n- When you iterate from position 63 down, you find bit [2] is set\n- You calculate `64 - 2 = 62` as the rho value, which is WRONG!\n- The actual rho should be around 61 (for bit position 61 in the original 64-bit value)\n\n### The Fix\n\nChange your implementation to work directly with the numeric value, not the bitset:\n\n```cpp\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const hash_t &hash) const -> uint64_t {\n  if (hash == 0) {\n    return 65;  // All zeros case: 64 leading zeros + 1\n  }\n  \n  // Use GCC built-in to count leading zeros\n  // __builtin_clzll(x) = number of leading zero bits in unsigned long long\n  // For a value with leftmost 1 at position k, there are (63 - k) leading zeros\n  // So rho = (63 - leading_zeros) + 1 = 64 - leading_zeros\n  int leading_zeros = __builtin_clzll(hash);\n  return 64 - leading_zeros;\n}\n```\n\nWait, let me reconsider this formula:\n- If `hash = 1` (binary: 000...0001), leading_zeros = 63, rho should = 64\n- Using `64 - 63 = 1` ❌ Wrong!\n\nThe correct formula is:\n- `rho = leading_zeros + 1`\n- If `hash = 1`, leading_zeros = 63, rho = 64 ✓\n- If `hash = (1ULL << 62)`, leading_zeros = 1, rho = 2 ✓\n- If `hash = (1ULL << 63)`, leading_zeros = 0, rho = 1 ✓\n\nHere's the corrected version:\n\n```cpp\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const hash_t &hash) const -> uint64_t {\n  if (hash == 0) {\n    return 65;  // All zeros case\n  }\n  \n  // __builtin_clzll counts leading zeros (0-63 for 64-bit unsigned value)\n  return __builtin_clzll(hash) + 1;\n}\n```\n\n### Summary of Key Points\n\n1. **Count leading zeros from MSB**: Use `__builtin_clzll()` to count leading zero bits\n2. **Add 1 to get rho**: The position of the first 1 bit = leading_zeros + 1\n3. **Special case for zero**: If all bits are zero (remaining_bits == 0), rho = 65\n4. **Work with numeric values, not bitsets**: Don't use bitset for this computation; work with the raw hash value\n5. **Rho range**: For a 64-bit hash, rho ranges from 1 (MSB is 1) to 65 (all zeros)\n\n### Test Your Understanding\n\nGiven these hash values (after extracting remaining bits):\n- `0x8000000000000000` (MSB = 1) → leading_zeros = 0 → **rho = 1**\n- `0x4000000000000000` (bit 62 = 1) → leading_zeros = 1 → **rho = 2**\n- `0x0000000000000001` (bit 0 = 1) → leading_zeros = 63 → **rho = 64**\n- `0x0000000000000000` (all zeros) → **rho = 65**\n\nThe key insight: **HyperLogLog needs to distinguish between hashes with different positions of their first 1 bit, and rho measures how many leading zeros come before that first 1 bit** (plus one).  // 2^n_bits, so 2^0=1, 2^1=2, etc.\n    registers_.assign(num_buckets_, 0);\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeBinary(const hash_t &hash) const -> std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> {\n  return std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY>(hash);\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> &bset) const -> uint64_t {\n  // Count leading zeros (from the most significant bit)\n  // and return the position of the first 1 bit\n  for (int64_t i = BITSET_CAPACITY - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n    if (bset[i]) {\n      return BITSET_CAPACITY - i;  // Number of leading zeros + 1\n    }\n  }\n  return BITSET_CAPACITY + 1;  // All zeros case\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::AddElem(KeyType val) -> void {\n  if (num_buckets_ == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(mutex_);\n\n  // Hash the value\n  hash_t hash_val = CalculateHash(val);\n\n  // Extract bucket index from the LSBs (least significant bits)\n  size_t bucket_idx = hash_val & ((1UL << n_bits_) - 1);\n\n  // Get the remaining bits after removing the bucket index bits\n  hash_t remaining_bits = hash_val >> n_bits_;\n\n  // Find position of leftmost 1 bit (rho value) in remaining_bits\n  // rho = number of leading zeros + 1\n  uint64_t rho;\n  if (remaining_bits == 0) {\n    rho = 65;  // All zeros case: 64 leading zeros + 1\n  } else {\n    // __builtin_clzll counts leading zeros in a 64-bit value\n    rho = __builtin_clzll(remaining_bits) + 1;\n  }\n\n  // Update the register with the maximum rho value\n  if (rho > registers_[bucket_idx]) {\n    registers_[bucket_idx] = rho;\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeCardinality() -> void {\n  if (num_buckets_ == 0) {\n    cardinality_ = 0;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(mutex_);\n\n  // HyperLogLog formula: E = constant * m^2 / sum(2^(-M[j]))\n  double m = static_cast<double>(num_buckets_);\n  double raw_estimate = 0.0;\n\n  for (size_t i = 0;  // 2^n_leading_bits, so 2^0=1, 2^1=2, etc.\n    dense_bucket_.assign(num_buckets_, 0);\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLogPresto<KeyType>::AddElem(KeyType val) -> void {\n  if (num_buckets_ == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(mutex_);\n\n  // Hash the value\n  hash_t hash_val = CalculateHash(val);\n\n  // Extract bucket index from the first n_leading_bits\n  size_t bucket_idx = hash_val & ((1UL << n_leading_bits_) - 1);\n\n  // Get the remaining bits after removing the bucket index bits\n  hash_t remaining_bits = hash_val >> n_leading_bits_;\n\n  // Count leading zeros in the remaining bits\n  uint64_t rho = 1;\n  if (remaining_bits != 0) {\n    rho = __builtin_clzll(remaining_bits) + 1;\n  } else {\n    rho = 65; i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n    uint64_t rho = dense_bucket_[i].to_ullong();\n\n    // Check if there's an overflow bucket value\n    if (overflow_bucket_.find(i) != overflow_bucket_.end()) {\n      // The overflow bucket stores additional rho value beyond the dense bucket\n      uint64_t overflow_val = overflow_bucket_[i].to_ullong();\n      // Use the max of dense and overflow\n      rho = std::max(rho, overflow_val);\n    }\n\n    // Clamp rho to reasonable values\n    if (rho == 0) {\n      rho = 1;\n    }\n\n    raw_estimate += std::pow(2.0, -static_cast<double>(rho));\n  }\n\n  raw_estimate = CONSTANT * m * m / raw_estimate;\n\n  // Small range correction\n  if (raw_estimate <= 2.5 * m) {\n    size_t zeros = 0;\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < num_buckets_; i++) {\n      if (dense_bucket_[i].to_ullong() == 0 && overflow_bucket_.find(i) == overflow_bucket_.end()) {\n        zeros++;\n      }\n    }\n    if (zeros != 0) {\n      raw_estimate = m * std::log(static_cast<double>(m) / static_cast<double>(zeros));\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Large range correction\n  if (raw_estimate > (1.0 / 30.0) * (1UL << 32)) {\n    raw_estimate = -1.0 * (1UL << 32) * std::log(1.0 - raw_estimate / (1UL << 32));\n  }\n\n  cardinality_ = static_cast<uint64_t>(raw_estimate + 0.5); The original design chains these together: hash → bitset → find leftmost one position.\n\nBut there's a fundamental problem with this approach—the bitset constructor doesn't preserve bit positions from the original hash value, so the iteration won't give the correct result. My __builtin_clzll method directly counts leading zeros in the hash itself, which should be more reliable if implemented correctly.\n\nIn AddElem, I'm updating registers only when rho exceeds the current value, which makes sense since registers start at 0.
